skill.name: azure-eventhub-rust
skill.description: Reference guide for using the Azure Event Hubs Rust SDK to send, receive, batch, and inspect streaming events.
skill.summary: Use Azure Event Hubs from Rust with producer, consumer, batching, partition, and checkpointing examples.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged shell-style fenced examples, Rust SDK calls, documentation URLs, and a weak-crypto blocker at SKILL.md:3. Manual review found these are documentation examples and metadata, with no executable skill scripts, prompt injection, credential exfiltration, or malicious intent. Publication is acceptable with a low-risk note because the skill includes install commands and external reference links.

low_findings.title: Fenced Shell Installation Commands
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported external command execution, but the evidence is Markdown code fences showing cargo add commands. These examples are not executable skill logic, but users should review dependency installation before running them.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines are fenced shell snippets in documentation. They install named Azure Rust crates and do not contain dynamic input, obfuscation, or chained shell behavior.

low_findings.title: External Documentation Links
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported hardcoded URLs. The links point to docs.rs, GitHub source code, and crates.io references for the documented Rust crate.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible reference links in a documentation table. I found no evidence that the skill sends data to these URLs or performs hidden network requests.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Finding Dismissed
low_findings.description: The static blocker at SKILL.md:3 is a false positive. The line begins YAML description metadata and does not name or use a weak cryptographic algorithm.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is metadata syntax for a description block. No cryptographic API, algorithm name, digest computation, or security-sensitive operation appears there.

dangerous_patterns.title: Documentation Code Blocks Resembling Commands
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill contains shell and Rust examples that static analysis classified as command execution. Manual review found documentation context only.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern exists in Markdown examples, but there is no executable wrapper, script file, or instruction to run hidden commands.

dangerous_patterns.title: Visible External Reference URLs
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes hardcoded URLs for public documentation and package references.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are public reference resources and are not used for telemetry, downloading scripts, or transmitting user data.

content.user_title: Build Azure Event Hubs Rust Clients
content.value_statement: Rust developers need clear examples for Azure Event Hubs producers and consumers. This skill provides focused SDK guidance for sending, receiving, batching, and inspecting event streams.
actual_capabilities: Shows how to add Azure Event Hubs Rust SDK dependencies.
Defines required Event Hubs namespace and hub name environment variables.
Provides producer client setup and single-event send examples.
Provides batch creation and batch send examples.
Shows consumer setup, partition receiving, and property lookup examples.
Mentions optional blob checkpoint store support for distributed consumers.
limitations: Does not include a full runnable Rust project structure.
Does not cover authentication setup beyond DeveloperToolsCredential usage.
Does not provide retry, error handling, or production deployment patterns.
Does not validate Event Hubs permissions, networking, or Azure resource configuration.

use_cases.title: Create a Rust Event Producer
use_cases.description: Build a service that sends individual events or batches to an Azure Event Hub.

use_cases.title: Read Events by Partition
use_cases.description: Prototype a Rust consumer that receives events from a selected Event Hub partition.

use_cases.title: Plan Event Hub Integration
use_cases.description: Review SDK concepts, dependency names, and checkpointing options before implementation.

prompt_templates.title: Install Dependencies
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me add the Rust dependencies needed for Azure Event Hubs and explain what each package is used for.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Producer
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me how to create an Azure Event Hubs producer in Rust and send one event to my hub.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Consumer
prompt_templates.prompt: Guide me through creating a Rust consumer that reads events from partition 0 and prints event bodies safely.

prompt_templates.title: Design Distributed Consumption
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me design a Rust Event Hubs consumer architecture with parallel partition processing and checkpoint storage.

output_examples.input: I need to send telemetry from a Rust service to Azure Event Hubs.
output_examples.output: A concise producer setup plan with dependency names, environment variables, credential setup, and event send flow.

output_examples.input: I need to consume events from one partition for a proof of concept.
output_examples.output: A partition receiver walkthrough that explains client creation, partition selection, receive limits, and event body inspection.

output_examples.input: I need durable consumption across several workers.
output_examples.output: A design outline covering consumer groups, parallel partitions, checkpointing, and the optional blob checkpoint store crate.

best_practices: Reuse Event Hubs clients instead of recreating them for every send or receive operation.
Use batches for higher throughput and check batch capacity before sending.
Use consumer groups and checkpointing when multiple consumers process streams.
anti_patterns: Do not hardcode production namespace names, hub names, or credentials in source code.
Do not ignore batch capacity failures when adding event data.
Do not run multiple consumers in the same group without a partition and checkpointing plan.

faq.question: What Rust crate does this skill focus on?
faq.answer: It focuses on azure_messaging_eventhubs, with azure_identity for authentication examples.

faq.question: Can this skill help send batches of events?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes batch creation, event addition, and batch send guidance.

faq.question: Does it cover receiving events?
faq.answer: Yes. It shows a consumer client and partition receiver flow for reading events.

faq.question: Does it include checkpointing?
faq.answer: It mentions the optional Azure Blob checkpoint store crate, but it does not provide a full checkpointing implementation.

faq.question: Does it manage Azure resources?
faq.answer: No. It assumes an Event Hubs namespace and event hub already exist.

faq.question: Is this suitable for production code generation?
faq.answer: It is a starting reference. Production systems still need error handling, retries, observability, and security review.
